10.reason: [The adjacency list `p[n][i]` is not guaranteed to be sorted in ascending order during DFS recursion because the loop iterates over fixed 100 slots, and deleted entries are marked as -1 but not skipped properly in traversal order. Also, this may access invalid vertices beyond actual neighbors.]

21.reason: [Condition `top-front>=0` is always true initially and doesn't correctly represent queue emptiness; should be `front <= top` or similar. This can cause out-of-bounds access when `front` exceeds valid indices.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect queue condition: should be 'front <= top' or similar; current condition may cause out-of-bounds access or infinite loop. Also, BFS prints node when dequeuing but marks visited when enqueuing inconsistently with DFS behavior.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Condition `top-front>=0` is always true initially and doesn't correctly represent queue emptiness; should be `front <= top` or similar. This can cause out-of-bounds access when `front` exceeds valid indices.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.2 , reason:  [This condition should be 'front <= top' or 'front <= top && front < 100', but as written, when front > top, top - front becomes negative, so loop stops correctly. However, the real error is that 'front' is incremented unconditionally even when no new nodes are added, and the BFS accesses p[front][i] where 'front' is an index into the queue, not the vertex label. It should use list[front] as the current vertex, not 'front' itself.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Condition should be 'front <= top' or similar; current condition may cause infinite loop or out-of-bounds access]

78.reason: [Bubble sort implementation is flawed: inner loop condition may access p[i][k+1] beyond valid range if last element is -1 but previous isn't handled correctly. Sorting may not complete fully due to early termination.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.25 , reason:  [Bubble sort implementation is flawed: inner loop condition may access p[i][k+1] beyond valid range if last element is -1 but previous isn't handled correctly. Sorting may not complete fully due to early termination.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.25 , reason:  [Bubble sort implementation is incomplete and inefficient; it only does one pass, so the list may not be fully sorted in ascending order]

110.reason: [Only removes the vertex value from adjacency lists but doesn't shift remaining elements left, leaving gaps (-1 values in middle), which breaks traversal logic that assumes contiguous valid neighbors until first -1.]

25.reason: [Here, 'front' is the queue index, not the vertex. Should be p[list[front]][i] instead of p[front][i]. This causes accessing wrong adjacency lists, leading to incorrect BFS traversal.]
